author: nero_eth
content:
up until now it feels there's support for this latest change. It's merged alread and I'd say we then also go with it if there's no opposition with good arguments against.
cc <@429719902363058177> <@680337202521440470> <@281967554572976128> <@628018527470616606> <@902806914252021830>

author: b.hartnett
content: Yeah we agree with this change. Nimbus EL's EVM already worked this way so that the delegated address wouldn't appear in the BAL when prior checks fail. It makes sense to do the minimal state lookups and the BAL should only include the state read/touched.

author: omerfirmak
content: please keep in mind that the PR from <@774033563732541451> needs to be extended to cover top level calls as well (process_message_call). Which currently behaves differently than inner calls. There might be a gap in tests wrt top level calls as well.

author: draganrakita
content: It is discrepancy, we are adding eip7702 delegation to the access list, while the account is not loaded in BAL. This is especially hard in revm as access list is not separate structure in Journal but the State map that is used to store account is used as access list, that same structure is used to create BAL. It is most performant way to handle journal access.

author: draganrakita
content: Could we move access list inclusion to be after those check for caller balance and call depth? It would require some workaround for us but it is possible to do.

author: mirgee
content: It is a simple and reasonable rule to record state access only when access is necessary and Besu already works this way, so I think Besu is OK with the change.

author: omerfirmak
content: so the delegation cost is deducted from inner frame's gas?

author: draganrakita
content: Just adding it to access list is delayed after those checks

author: omerfirmak
content: That is fine too, but moving the cost as well makes more sense to me. But it might be a worse breaking change.

author: draganrakita
content: It is strange, that now child call needs to arbitrary pay for gas.

author: draganrakita
content: For revm, charging gas and then loading it again would be basically mean an additional hashmap, but that should be okay.

author: nero_eth
content: So, geth, besu, reth and nimbus are all supportive to move along with this change.

author: nero_eth
content: Nethermind and ethrex signaled to be in support too.

author: draganrakita
content: I would not like to move towards it, but only way to make it work (if majority wants is) is to move inclusion of it in access list after those checks.

author: draganrakita
content: <@792404665068158998> if others wants it, then we should include it. But for revm to be able to work (without major rework), we need to access list to be moved.

author: nero_eth
content: So, you're fine with the EIPs changes or would you move any charge/access differently?

author: draganrakita
content: For us to support it, we need to move inclusion to access list after those checks.

author: nero_eth
content: But that's what the new changes propose no?

author: nero_eth
content: The delegate address is only added after all checks pass

author: draganrakita
content: Maybe I missed it then. I think I assumed adding it to access list is different notion from adding it to BAL. If they are same then than is great!

author: draganrakita
content: So we still want to charge gas for warm/cold load before those checks, but do the cold load, adding to access list and BAL after those checks.

author: omerfirmak
content: I believe your initial understanding was correct. In spec, warming up of the delegate addr still happens before the check.

author: draganrakita
content: <@792404665068158998> wdyt about moving warming after check? <@126385519469789184> you said you are okay with it

author: nero_eth
content: Would this be a separate change, as this behavior is defined in 7702?

author: nero_eth
content: Thinking about it, it's just not specified bc not relevant until BAL.

author: nero_eth
content:
Is it that what you'd want?
https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/commit/3b7a8f15fc8ac4448c0e6749b017df63824395d7
